rationale: In the Data and Operationalizations section, the citation is tied to concrete methodological choices rather than a generic literature list. The authors explicitly note using a similar approach to Geyskens et al. for keeping households present throughout the panel and for selecting top-selling brands. This gives the cited paper a clear functional role in how the empirical analysis is set up, even though its private-label theory is not used for hypothesis development.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Geyskens et al. (2010) as an empirical-design precedent when constructing its dataset, specifically for household panel retention and brand-selection rules. It applies these data-handling conventions in a different context, namely promotion calendar effectiveness.
